title: Jovibarba sobolifera
taxonomy: Jovibarba sobolifera is a species of flowering plant in the saxifrage family (Saxifragaceae). It is a succulent plant, native to mountainous regions in Europe. Jovibarba sobolifera is also known by its common name, the cluster-cup saxifrage. This plant produces small, yellow flowers in the summer months. It is a low-growing, mat-forming species that is often used in alpine gardens for its attractive foliage.

intro: Jovibarba sobolifera, commonly known as the alpine bear's head or alpine bear's ear, is a low-growing, succulent plant species native to the Arctic tundra and high-altitude mountains of Europe. Jovibarba sobolifera has been traditionally used for its purported health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to soothe digestive issues and alleviate respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and polysaccharides, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Jovibarba sobolifera include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support immune function and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
